title: HTTP Client Ownership, Context, Status, Body Limits, and Cleanup · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent conservation group receives a different region, command sequence, API fixture, cache age, concurrency budget, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers http client ownership, context, status, body limits, and cleanup into a changed reusable bounded context-aware PokéAPI client. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of reusable bounded context-aware PokéAPI client with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through pred…
objectives: Inject and reuse one configured http.Client or narrow transport port because transports retain connection state and support concurrent use.
Create requests with caller-owned context and derive request deadlines without hiding cancellation behind Background.
Classify success, not found, client, server, redirect, and unexpected status before domain decoding and retain bounded error evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Pokedex Go Client Reuse
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 2305 during a race-instrumented cache stress case. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove client reuse, response admission, bounded bodies, context cancellation, and cleanup; browser code must remain deterministic and side-effect-free; then change one catch, owned record, encounter relation, or history state and prove the prior invariant. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?
